title: Family Finance App — Product Context
product_perspective: A standalone native Android application for personal/family financial tracking. Intended as a B2C SaaS product with potential to scale to thousands of users. No dependency on external financial APIs at MVP; data is stored locally (Room) with Supabase cloud sync added post-stabilization.

product_scope.description: A shared-vault family finance tracker allowing multiple family members to manage accounts (bank, cash, credit card, investment), log income and categorized expenses, split receipts, tag transactions to projects/trips, and run basic financial reports.
in_scope: Create/Edit/View Accounts (Bank, Cash, Investment, Credit Card)
Record Expenses with Category, Account, Date, Amount, Receiver
Fast receipt-splitting UI (single-screen, dynamic split lines)
Project/Trip tagging for grouped expense reporting
Timeline view (chronological transactions grouped by month)
Minimal Visualizations: Timeline total, Cash vs Non-Cash, Expenses by Category per month
out_of_scope: Strict inter-family privacy / Row Level Security between spouses
Live investment ticker tracking (manual balance updates only)
Automated currency exchange rate API lookup
User authentication and Family Group invitations (deferred with Supabase sync)

operating_environment: Android smartphones running Android 8.0 (API 26) and above. Primary development and CI on Windows with Android Gradle build system. Backend: Supabase (PostgreSQL) — used post-MVP.

definitions.definition: A privacy model where all family members see all accounts and transactions. Owner labels are used for quick filtering rather than strict data isolation.
A single physical receipt recorded as two or more distinct expense rows in the database, each with its own Category, all linked via a shared receipt_group_id UUID.
An optional grouping tag (e.g., 'Lulea Trip') that can be attached to transactions to enable specific cross-category financial reporting.
A movement of funds between two internal accounts. Stored as two linked TransactionEntity rows (debit + credit) sharing a transfer_linked_id UUID.
A string field on accounts and transactions indicating which family member owns or created the record, used for filtering, not for security enforcement.
